So, for example, in Europe, PAVE uses 10% sample data from direct connections to an HR system.
We use 90% direct connection real-time data.
So they're using mostly differentials and extrapolations based on their U.S.
data to inform Europe.
So we are truly best fit.
So the flywheel is like...
To see the data, you've got to connect your data, and then it kind of just keeps building.
It is the flywheel.
It's also our greatest objection, because buyers are worried about, you know, it's HR.
Everything is sensitive.
Everything is privacy, especially in Europe, where there's a lot more regulation around personal data.
And so learning, understanding what the buyer needs to see and believe to trust the quality of our data without that integration, that can exist, and that can be, that can exist in our PLG motion.
So it's been really fun.
And then, like, where does this go, you think?
Like, like, is it converged with, like, ATSs and recruiters?
Like, where does, like, the company go?
Yeah, so we do integrate with ATS platforms as well.
And one of my objectives going into 2026 is to explore recruiters as an entry point.
In our buying committee.

So what I mean by that is if we start targeting recruiters first, get them hooked on our ATS integrations to have new hire benchmarks, there's actually more urgency in the context of their job to be done because they need to close a candidate quickly.
Whereas when we're talking about the broader compensation reviews, it's a longer timeframe of the job to be done.
So there's less urgency.
So if your comp review is happening in September or October, and you're looking and evaluating data providers in February, there's less urgency there, but recruiters have it.

So if we can get them hooked in becoming an advocate for Ravio in their context, they'll do the influencing for us, which sounds very similar to what you're doing, almost on a user level with community, who can then become the ambassador and influence your decision makers for a later upsell.
Yeah, no, that makes sense.
And then like, but it's the goal for the company to kind of, like, I guess, eventually to be the like, canonical.

Source of compensation data, and then you're kind of just selling that to everyone, they have to come to you, or is the plan to like take that and build something on top of the, you know, that is more valuable, potentially?
Great question.
So we have been going through a positioning exercise, more existential exercise, because over the past two years, we have built out end-to-end workflow tools that sit on top of the data.

So salary bans, identifying pay equity gaps, running and facilitating a compensation review, all leverage real-time market data, but are sitting on top of it, making us a more one-to-one direct competitor with PAVE.
However, that's not the reason the company was founded.
The company was founded to be a data company.
And so I've just been working with the founders on repositioning Ravio and doubling down on the quality of our data.
And the positioning is basically Rabio as a data backbone for any compensation decision.
There's so much open ocean of folks who are currently asking peers how to benchmark a role using self-reported data, which is less reliable.
So it's a big open ocean.
If we just stay within this kind of more narrow but deepening of our proposition, I think it's actually a much more sustainable path to growth.
Yeah, no, that makes sense.

It's just like, I feel like everyone in SF, like their heads are in the cloud a little bit, you know, in terms of like, thinking about what to build, because like, when I look at what Rabio is doing, I guess the mindset that I bring is like the same one with growthx of like, like AI is doing something crazy to every space.
And what seems to be happening is like, every space is converging at this, like, like, everyone's trying to replace the person.
Because like, if you you're it's
If follow the money, like your spend in any department is the people there.
And 70% of operating costs.
Yeah.
It's all thing.
I'm sorry, I think I disconnected.
Yeah, but it seems like the path to building a more valuable thing is like figuring out the path to like charge for the work that a person is doing.
So, yeah, I don't know.
Like our version of that is like, I mean, it feels like you can charge for marketing work and productize that, you know, and frame it almost like you're hiring a marketer.

I feel like customer success seems to be the furthest along because it's like the most kind of repeatable, measurable, and like regular thing, like the Crestos and Decagons and I don't know, like Pylon and Sierra, like...
I think they're the first along of just like, man, I can just kind of like fire half my customer support team and get an agent to send that a loop.

And then maybe it's like, I think sales is somewhere there, but sales is inherently weird where it's like, if you do it really well, it becomes more noisy and it's just like this constant back and forth.
like 11x is like somewhat, you know, like made a really  BDR kind of like an AI version of it, right?

it sounds like you do already have marketing folks in place.

then it's like once a piece of that system becomes like mature enough, I think then it's like, okay, how do you kind of like build that, build that out?

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[To explore a potential marketing leadership role at GrowthX for Alicia Carney.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=3172.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**GrowthX's Challenge:** The agency hit a scaling wall (waitlist until Feb) because its white-glove service is unproductizable. The core challenge is building a GTM flywheel to convert its large community audience into customers for its new productized offerings.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=998.0)
  - [**Alicia's Motivation:** Seeking a new role at an AI-native company. Ravio (her current company) is successful ($5M ARR) but lacks AI curiosity, and she misses the high-intensity environment of her Deliveroo days.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1841.0)
  - [**Strategic Alignment:** Alicia's experience building a community-led GTM at Ravio and her product marketing background directly address GrowthX's need for a strategic leader to unify its disparate marketing efforts.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=2672.0)
  - [**The Role:** A generalist marketing leader to build the GTM system, leveraging a product marketing foundation to create compelling "offers" and connect the community, events, and product into a cohesive flywheel.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=3187.0)

## Topics

### GrowthX's Business Model & Challenge

  - [**Mission:** Define the future of knowledge work using AI.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=134.0)
  - [**Strategy:** Use a white-glove agency to discover what "good looks like," then productize the scalable components.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=177.0)
  - [**Current State:**](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=214.0)
      - [**Agency:** High-touch service for Series B+ B2B SaaS.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=523.0)
          - [**Success:** $12-15M revenue in \~1 year, 100 people.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=255.0)
          - [**Challenge:** Unscalable due to unproductizable human elements (e.g., CMO alignment).](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=488.0)
      - [**Community:** A paid learning hub ($30/mo, 200+ members) that serves as the primary GTM channel.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1020.0)
      - [**Platform:** A new product launching soon (mid-hundreds/mo) to track AI visibility and guide strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1044.0)
  - [**Core Problem:** The agency is at capacity (waitlist until Feb). The key challenge is building a scalable GTM flywheel to convert the community audience into customers for the new platform.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=998.0)

### Alicia's Background & Motivation

  - [**Experience:** 14 years in product marketing (PMM), transitioning to Head of Marketing.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1273.0)
      - [**Ravio:** Head of Marketing at the compensation data platform ($5M ARR, 50 people).](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1878.0)
          - [**Achievement:** Built a community-led GTM, shifting budget from performance ads to a more cost-efficient channel.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1368.0)
      - [**Deliveroo:** Experience in a hyper-growth environment.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1285.0)
  - [**Motivation for Change:**](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1841.0)
      - [**AI Interest:** Strong passion for AI; Ravio lacks internal AI curiosity.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=2409.0)
      - [**Pace:** Seeks the intensity and scale of a Deliveroo-like environment.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1896.0)
      - [**Location:** Desire to be more connected to the SF tech scene.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1850.0)

### The Proposed Marketing Role

  - [**The Gap:** GrowthX's marketing team is composed of specialists (Events, Content, Production). The missing piece is a generalist leader to build the overarching GTM system.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1194.0)
  - [**Core Responsibilities:**](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=3187.0)
      - [**System Building:** Connect the community, events, and product into a cohesive flywheel.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=1224.0)
      - [**Offer Creation:** Leverage a product marketing foundation to create compelling "offers" that deliver value and drive the customer journey.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=2816.0)
      - [**Strategic Judgment:** Select and execute marketing channels with a clear purpose, avoiding unnecessary complexity.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=3268.0)
  - [**Strategic Alignment:** Alicia's experience building community-led GTMs and her product marketing background are a direct fit for this challenge.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=2672.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Jason:** Send Alicia a calendar invite for a follow-up call.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=3123.0)
  - [**Jason:** Send Alicia details on the 2-3 hour take-home exercise.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=3123.0)
  - [**Jason:** Invite Marcel to the final discussion call.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=3123.0)
  - [**Alicia:** Send Jason salary benchmarks for the role.](https://fathom.video/share/72tczTe4Zb19g-t6qHPQTEnYkwF_QS5Y?tab=summary&timestamp=3531.0)
